question_text: Which of the following statements correctly describes the relationship between voltage, current, and resistance in a circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: If voltage increases and resistance stays the same, the current will decrease.

options.label: B
options.text: If voltage increases and resistance stays the same, the current will increase.

options.label: C
options.text: Voltage and current are not related to each other in a circuit.

options.label: D
options.text: If resistance increases, the current will increase even if voltage stays the same.

correct_answer: B) If voltage increases and resistance stays the same, the current will increase.
explanation: Ohm's law (V = IR) shows that current is directly proportional to voltage when resistance is constant. So if voltage increases, current must increase to maintain the relationship. Option A is the opposite of what happens, option C ignores Ohm's law, and option D shows an inverse relationship that is incorrect.
